Voter Registration Vote By Mail Home Government Elections Office Elections Office About the Office Hours M-F 8:30 - 4:30 Phone 570-296-3427 Internal Extension 1094 Address 506 Broad Street Milford, PA 18337 Department Head Nadeen Manzoni Pike County May 19, 2026 General Primary Election Canvass of the Vote The Pike County Board of Elections will meet in the Commissioners Conference Room on the first floor of the Administration Building, located at 506 Broad Street, Milford, Pike County Pennsylvania, at on Friday, May 22, 2026 following the General Primary  Election, to publicly commence and hold the session for the computation and canvassing of the election returns from the various election precincts of Pike County and continue the same from day-to-day until completed. At the same day and time the Board of Elections will canvass the Provisional Ballots cast in this election. CLICK HERE FOR ELECTION RESULTS! Results Per Precinct Summary Reports ACT 88 NOTICE In accordance with Act 88 reporting requirements, Pike County received a total of 3,932 mail-in and absentee ballots by 8pm on Election Day, May 19, 2026. The Elections Office maintains and controls all activities relating to Voter Registration and Elections under the supervision of the Pike County Board of Elections. The office prepares machine ballots, programs voting machines, processes, and issues absentee ballots, oversees district election workers, and maintains county and local candidate filings for elected offices. In addition, the office also maintains records of county election results, campaign finance filings, polling places, and district election workers. Voter Hall of Fame The Pike County Board of Elections Office inducted six Pike County voters into the Pennsylvania Voter Hall of Fame at the July 17, 2024 Pike County Commissioners Meeting. In Pennsylvania, a voter that has voted in 50 consecutive elections is eligible to be entered into the Pennsylvania Voter Hall of Fame and to receive a certificate from the Governor commending this achievement.
